stories.title: Iran submits 14-point response to U.S. proposal to end war
stories.description: Iran has presented a 14-point response to the U.S. proposal to end the war, according to Iranian state media.

stories.title: Collaboration helps NPR journalists get you the story of the Iran war up close
stories.description: Three NPR journalists talk about the challenge of producing independent, accurate coverage of the war with Iran.

stories.title: ICRC President Mirjana Spoljaric warns of the war's impact on civilians
stories.description: The ICRC president talks about her recent trip to Iran, and warns about the impact of the Iran war on civilians.

stories.title: Imprisoned Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner in critical condition
stories.description: Narges Mohammadi, an Iranian activist and 2023 Nobel Peace Prize laureate, was hospitalized after collapsing in prison. Her family says her condition has deteriorated since a March heart attack.

stories.title: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
stories.description: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

stories.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
stories.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

stories.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
stories.description: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.description: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: Watch: There's a ceasefire in Lebanon - but the fighting hasn't stopped
stories.description: Israel and Hezbollah are still fighting in Lebanon despite a US-brokered ceasefire that began 12 days ago.

stories.title: Southern Lebanon: Israeli strikes kill one, wounds rescuers
stories.description: Israel has carried out fresh air strikes in southern Lebanon. Officials there said at least one person was killed and four others were wounded. The strikes came after the Israel Defense Forces issued new evacuation warnings on Sunday for residents in more than ten villages and to...

stories.title: US President Trump says not likely to accept Iranian proposal
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said Saturday he was going to review a new Iranian peace proposal but said he doubted it would be acceptable, saying that Tehran had "not yet paid a big enough price". Trump’s comments came after Iran's Tasnim and Fars news agencies said Tehran had submi...

stories.title: Inflation surging past 50% in Iran
stories.description: The fragile ceasefire may have paused the fighting, but the economic fallout in Iran continues. A US naval blockade of Iranian ports has sharply reduced the Iran’s oil exports, while the cost of everyday goods has continued to rise. Across the country, people are now bracing for ...

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalised
stories.description: Iran’s imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been urgently transferred from prison to hospital after what her foundation described as a severe deterioration in her health. She reportedly fainted twice on Friday. The incident follows a suspected heart attack i...

stories.title: New Iranian proposal to end the war under examination
stories.description: A senior Iranian officer on Saturday said that fresh fighting with the United States is “likely”, underscoring rising tensions after President Donald Trump dismissed Tehran’s latest negotiating proposal and issued fresh threats to attack the country. Daniel Quinlan reports.

stories.title: Bulk carrier attacked by multiple small craft off Iran, UKMTO says
stories.description: Crew is safe and no environmental impact has been reported after the incident 11 nautical miles (20km) west of Sirik.

stories.title: US to cut troops in Germany as Iran war spat deepens
stories.description: US-German relations are straining as a spat deepens between President Trump and Chancellor Merz over the war on Iran.

stories.title: Was the Iran war the final blow in the collapse of Spirit Airlines?
stories.description: The shutdown of the budget airline after 34 years has left 17,000 staff members unemployed.

stories.title: OPEC+ announces symbolic oil output rise during Strait of Hormuz closure
stories.description: Seven major producers will add 188,000 barrels per day to their total production quota for June, OPEC+ says.

stories.title: What’s Iran’s 14-point proposal to end the war? And will Trump accept it?
stories.description: Trump is reviewing Iran's latest proposal to end the war, but the mistrust between the two sides is a big obstacle.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade and other major naval sieges in modern times
stories.description: Naval blockades are among the oldest weapons of war, using sea control to cut supplies and force an enemy’s surrender.

stories.title: Iran reviewing US response to 14-point proposal: state media
stories.description: Tehran said on Sunday that the United States had responded to its 14-point proposal via Pakistan and that it was reviewing the response, according to Iranian state media. “At this stage, we do not have nuclear negotiations,” an Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson said, accordin...

stories.title: US Secretary of State Rubio to visit Vatican, Rome after Trump's row with Pope
stories.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will visit Rome and the Vatican this week, an Italian government source said on Sunday, just weeks after a clash between Donald Trump and Pope Leo. Rubio, who is a Catholic, is expected to meet Vatican Secretary of State Pietro Parolin and Italia...

stories.title: Iran says ball in US court on war or diplomacy
stories.description: WASHINGTON: A man stands atop the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge on Saturday to protest the US-Israeli war on Iran. Guido Reichstadter climbed the bridge in Washington on May Day, stayed there overnight, and posted a sunrise photo from one of the bridge’s arches while calling...

stories.title: Israel approves plan to buy F-35 and F-15IA fighter jets from Lockheed, Boeing
stories.description: Israel gave final approval for a plan to purchase two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA advanced fighter aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing in a deal worth tens of billions of dollars, the defence ministry said on Sunday. The deal, approved by Israel’s Ministerial Com...

stories.title: Japan contains one of its biggest wildfires in decades after 11-day battle
stories.description: Wildfires that scorched forests in northern Japan, reportedly the second biggest in over 30 years, have been brought under control after 11 days, officials have said. Hundreds of firefighters and more than 1,000 military personnel had battled the blazes since late April, as they ...

stories.title: Bin Laden nearly slipped out of Abbottabad: CIA report
stories.description: • Al Qaeda leader had agreed in writing to relocate by September 2011• Two brothers sheltering him sought separation, citing exhaustion• Agency claims to be entirely unaware of the relocation plan, says delayed raid would have missed the opportunity WASHINGTON: A decade after the...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Iran says it is reviewing U.S. response to latest proposal
stories.description: Bulk carrier near Strait of Hormuz reportedly being attacked, U.K. military says

stories.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon, kills one and wounds rescuers
stories.description: The warning covered more than 10 villages and towns including several in the district of Nabatieh, which lies north of the Litani River, south of which Israel has stationed troops

stories.title: Between a fatwa and war: how Iran makes sense of its nuclear capability
stories.description: Iran has ‘vowed’ to protect its nuclear stockpile against Trump’s threats even as its former Ayatollah Ali Khamenei disapproved of using nuclear weapons on religious grounds

stories.title: Sleepless in Tehran as crowds rally behind the flag
stories.description: A slogan on a wall nearby in Farsi read, “Meydan bashuma, khayabane ba ma”. ‘The battlefield is yours. The streets are ours.’ Addressed to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), it was a message to the soldiers to focus on the battlefield with ordinary Iranians in control ...

stories.title: Iran says U.S. military operation ‘impossible’ as Trump mulls peace proposal
stories.description: The Guards intelligence organisation said that “U.S. President Donald Trump must choose between 'an impossible operation or a bad deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran'", in an online post carried by state television.

stories.title: OPEC+ hikes oil production quotas without mentioning UAE pull-out
stories.description: But raising the quota on paper may not have much impact on actual production, which is already short of the limit
